root_text.sn56.24:1.2: “Ye hi keci, bhikkhave, atītamaddhānaṁ arahanto sammāsambuddhā yathābhūtaṁ abhisambujjhiṁsu, sabbe te cattāri ariyasaccāni yathābhūtaṁ abhisambujjhiṁsu.
root_text.sn56.24:1.3: Ye hi keci, bhikkhave, anāgatamaddhānaṁ arahanto sammāsambuddhā yathābhūtaṁ abhisambujjhissanti, sabbe te cattāri ariyasaccāni yathābhūtaṁ abhisambujjhissanti.
root_text.sn56.24:1.4: Ye hi keci, bhikkhave, etarahi arahanto sammāsambuddhā yathābhūtaṁ abhisambujjhanti, sabbe te cattāri ariyasaccāni yathābhūtaṁ abhisambujjhanti.
root_text.sn56.24:2.2: Dukkhaṁ ariyasaccaṁ, dukkhasamudayaṁ ariyasaccaṁ, dukkhanirodhaṁ ariyasaccaṁ, dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā ariyasaccaṁ.
root_text.sn56.24:2.3: Ye hi, keci, bhikkhave, atītamaddhānaṁ arahanto sammāsambuddhā yathābhūtaṁ abhisambujjhiṁsu …pe…
root_text.sn56.24:2.5: abhisambujjhanti, sabbe te imāni cattāri ariyasaccāni yathābhūtaṁ abhisambujjhanti.
root_text.sn56.24:3.1: Tasmātiha, bhikkhave, ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yogo karaṇīyo …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yogo karaṇīyo”ti.

translation_text.sn56.24:1.2: “Mendicants, whatever perfected ones, fully awakened Buddhas truly awaken—in the past,
translation_text.sn56.24:1.4: or present—all of them truly awaken to the four noble truths.
translation_text.sn56.24:2.2: The noble truths of suffering, the origin of suffering, the cessation of suffering, and the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering.
translation_text.sn56.24:2.3: Whatever perfected ones, fully awakened Buddhas truly awaken—in the past,
translation_text.sn56.24:2.5: or present—all of them truly awaken to the four noble truths.
translation_text.sn56.24:3.1: That’s why you should practice meditation …”
